data.content_ref: inline:contradicts: ac8a8325664e posits that the collapse was triggered by the democratization of iron-working making the palace's bronze monopoly obsolete, whereas 83eb7701, b6371e, and 7edd1384 all posit the collapse was triggered by a systemic failure of the international bronze supply chain. The former requires bronze to be the dominant, valued material whose control is suddenly undermined by a superior alternative (iron), while the latter requires bronze to remain the dominant, valued material whose sudden scarcity causes systemic collapse. These mechanisms are mutually exclusive: iron cannot simultaneously be a superior, adopted substitute and an irrelevant factor in a crisis driven by bronze scarcity. Refuted if evidence shows the iron transition and the tin-supply disruption were not contemporaneous or were causally independent.
